nameMeanings.name: Pigg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English pigge ‘pig’ (Old English picg(a)), hence a metonymic occupational name for a swineherd or nickname for someone supposedly resembling a hog (see Hogg ).

variants.name: Hogg
meanings: English (northern England and Scotland): nickname for a swineherd or shepherd, from Middle English hog(ge) (Old English hogg) denoting either a pig, especially a castrated one, or a young sheep before its first shearing (the latter sense is most likely in northern England).
German (Högg): topographic name, a variant of Heck 1, found chiefly in Bavaria.

variants.name: Pigue
meanings: French (southern): probably a variant of   Pique   ‘pick’. This surname is very rare in France.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: William Pigg was born in Widdington, Essex, England, United Kingdom as the son of John Pigge Of Widdington and Margaret Saunders. He married Edith Cokayne about 1545, in Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died in May 1592, in Newport, Essex, Massachusetts, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: William Pigg est né à Widdington, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de John Pigge Of Widdington et Margaret Saunders. Il a épousé Edith Cokayne environ 1545, à Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en mai 1592, à Newport, Essex, Massachusetts, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: William Pigg wurde in Widdington, Essex, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von John Pigge Of Widdington und Margaret Saunders geboren. Er hat um 1545 in Essex, England, United Kingdom Edith Cokayne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb im Mai 1592 in Newport, Essex, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten.

ja.lifeSummary: William PiggはイギリスイギリスエセックスウィッドイントンでJohn Pigge Of WiddingtonとMargaret Saundersの息子として生まれた。約1545年にイギリスイギリスエセックスでEdith Cokayneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1592年5月にアメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州エセックスニュープオルトで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Pigg(은)는 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 위딩턴에서 John Pigge Of Widdington(와)과 Margaret Saunders의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1545년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스에서 Edith Cokayne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1592년 5월 미국 매사추세츠 주 에세스 뉴포트에서 사망했다.
